input: Create a Bluesky social client module. It should let a user authenticate with their Bluesky credentials, view their timeline feed, create new text posts, view user profiles, and follow/unfollow users. Use the @atproto/api package for the AT Protocol. Show post content with author display name, handle, avatar, and timestamp. Include like/unlike functionality on posts.
hint: Use @atproto/api BskyAgent class. Login via agent.login({ identifier, password }). Get timeline via agent.getTimeline(). Create post via agent.post({ text }). Get profile via agent.getProfile({ actor }). Follow via agent.follow(did). Like via agent.like(uri, cid). API base URL is https://bsky.social.

supports like/unlike on posts

input: Create a discussion forum module. It should support creating threads with title and body, replying to threads with nested replies (one level deep), upvoting/downvoting threads and replies, and sorting threads by newest, most upvoted, or most active. Show thread list with title, author, reply count, and vote score. Include a search bar for thread titles.
hint: Threads are top-level objects with a replies array. Vote score = upvotes - downvotes. Most active = most recent reply timestamp. Nested replies are one level deep (reply to thread, not reply to reply). Search filters thread titles case-insensitively.

input: Create a weather dashboard module. It should display current weather conditions (temperature, humidity, wind speed, description, icon) and a 5-day forecast. Accept a city name input to fetch weather data. Show temperature in both Celsius and Fahrenheit. Include a simple line chart of temperature trends across the forecast period. Use placeholder data structure compatible with OpenWeatherMap API format.
hint: Use OpenWeatherMap-compatible JSON structure: { main: { temp, humidity }, wind: { speed }, weather: [{ description, icon }] }. For forecast: array of 5 daily entries. Line chart can be SVG polyline. Temperature conversion: F = C * 9/5 + 32.

input: Create a color palette generator module. It should generate harmonious color palettes using color theory rules: complementary, analogous, triadic, split-complementary, and tetradic. Accept a base color via hex input or color picker. Display generated colors as swatches with hex, RGB, and HSL values. Support copying color values to clipboard. Include a contrast checker showing WCAG AA/AAA compliance between any two selected colors.
hint: Convert hex to HSL for harmony calculations. Complementary = +180° hue. Analogous = ±30° hue. Triadic = ±120° hue. Contrast ratio formula: (L1 + 0.05) / (L2 + 0.05) where L is relative luminance. WCAG AA requires 4.5:1 for normal text, AAA requires 7:1.

input: Create a simple drawing canvas module. input: Create a Newton's laws simulator module. It should include three interactive simulations: (1) a simple pendulum showing oscillation with adjustable length and gravity, (2) a projectile motion simulator with adjustable angle and initial velocity showing trajectory path, and (3) a spring-mass system with adjustable spring constant and mass. Display real-time values for position, velocity, and acceleration. Include play/pause/reset controls and speed adjustment.
hint: Pendulum: theta(t) via small-angle approximation or Euler integration. Projectile: x = v0*cos(a)*t, y = v0*sin(a)*t - 0.5*g*t^2. Spring: x(t) = A*cos(sqrt(k/m)*t). Render via requestAnimationFrame with Canvas 2D. Speed adjustment scales dt per frame.

simulates projectile motion with adjustable angle and velocity
simulates spring-mass system with adjustable spring constant and mass
displays real-time position, velocity, and acceleration values
provides play, pause, and reset controls
adjusts simulation speed
